theme: Color an interference graph with limited colors to minimize weighted spill of the uncolorable nodes.
title: Spill-Aware Register Coloring on a Budget
strategies: raw index-order first-fit: color nodes 0..n-1 in given order with the smallest free color, weight-blind (reproduces the evaluator's weak baseline, ~0.1)

DSATUR (saturation-degree-order) coloring, weight-blind: always color the most-saturated uncolored node next, tie-break by static degree then index; beats raw order generically but is trapped when a cheap dense cluster saturates first and strands an expensive hub node
weight-biased DSATUR: same saturation-degree order but tie-break by spill weight first, so expensive nodes get seated before a cheap cluster can consume every color around them

weight-biased DSATUR + Kempe-chain repair: after the initial pass, rescue any still-spilled node by flipping a Kempe chain (a connected 2-color-alternating component, always safe to flip) to free a blocking color, or by evicting a strictly cheaper blocking neighbor when no safe chain exists
